{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.3", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The gap statement and contribution claim are present but buried under hedging and subordinate clauses; improving the draft requires surfacing the thesis with precision and removing self-undermining qualifiers like 'we believe represents a significant gap'.",
    "R1.3": "The draft carries multiple load-bearing quantitative claims (22–42 pp collapse, 85%→50% deployment gap, ~45% unconstrained PPO, >99.6% model-based); all must be preserved exactly while prose is tightened around them.",
    "R1.5": "The strongest finding (>99.6% model-based commitment) is deferred to paragraph 3 behind weaker results; restructuring to lead with the gap, then the headline result, then the mechanism would sharpen the narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Load-bearing. The draft is dense with run-on sentences, redundant hedges ('we believe,' 'we think,' 'going forward,' 'somewhat,' 'in this study'), and appositional overload ('which is…which is…'); writing polish is the primary driver of the improvement request."
  },
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": ""
}

rationale.R1.1: The gap statement and contribution claim are present but buried under hedging and subordinate clauses; improving the draft requires surfacing the thesis with precision and removing self-undermining qualifiers like 'we believe represents a significant gap'.
rationale.R1.3: The draft carries multiple load-bearing quantitative claims (22–42 pp collapse, 85%→50% deployment gap, ~45% unconstrained PPO, >99.6% model-based); all must be preserved exactly while prose is tightened around them.
rationale.R1.5: The strongest finding (>99.6% model-based commitment) is deferred to paragraph 3 behind weaker results; restructuring to lead with the gap, then the headline result, then the mechanism would sharpen the narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing. The draft is dense with run-on sentences, redundant hedges ('we believe,' 'we think,' 'going forward,' 'somewhat,' 'in this study'), and appositional overload ('which is…which is…'); writing polish is the primary driver of the improvement request.
